flashcards.front: What is the formula for conditional probability?

flashcards.back: Two events A and B are independent if P(A∩B) = P(A) × P(B)
The probability of an event based on prior knowledge of related conditions

flashcards.front: In network reliability, why is independence crucial?
flashcards.back: It allows calculating system failure probability as product of component failures

flashcards.front: What's the multiplication rule for dependent events?
How to test independence using conditional probability?
flashcards.back: Check if P(A|B) = P(A) and P(B|A) = P(B)

flashcards.front: What's P(A|B) when A and B are mutually exclusive?

flashcards.back: Consecutive coin flips - outcome of one doesn't affect the next

flashcards.front: Why is conditional probability vital in ML?
flashcards.back: Forms basis of Naive Bayes classifiers and probabilistic models

flashcards.front: What's the law of total probability?
flashcards.back: P(A) = Σ P(A|B_i) × P(B_i) for partition {B_i}
Conditional independence: A⊥B|C. Independence: A⊥B unconditionally
Must satisfy pairwise and triple independence: P(A∩B∩C) = P(A)P(B)P(C)
